verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is canonically a type II Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase, but the long-form isoform has documented plasma-membrane localization with the catalytic domain exposed extracellularly, where it functions as a cell-surface recognition molecule binding oligosaccharide ligands on opposing cells/ECM (e.g., sperm-egg, migratory edge, intercellular junctions). UniProt explicitly annotates 'Cell membrane; Single-pass type II membrane protein' and 'Cell surface' alongside Golgi. This is a dual-localization case where the protein has its own TM anchor at the PM in a minority pool.
